name: OpenBSD TCP SACK
description: Signed integer overflow in 32-bit TCP sequence number comparison. Two bugs chained: missing lower-bound check + NULL pointer write when SACK block simultaneously deletes only hole and triggers append.

name: FFmpeg H.264 Integer Mismatch
description: 16-bit vs 32-bit integer mismatch in slice counter. Attacker crafts frame with exactly 65,536 slices to collide with 0xFFFF sentinel value, causing out-of-bounds heap write.

name: FreeBSD NFS Remote Code Execution
description: Stack buffer overflow in NFS server: 128-byte buffer, 32-byte header, 96 remaining, max copy of 400 bytes = 304 bytes overflow. 20-gadget ROP chain split over 6 sequential RPC packets. Appends attacker SSH key to /root/.ssh/authorized_keys.

name: VMM Guest-to-Host Memory Corruption
description: Bug in unsafe code of a memory-safe VMM. Out-of-bounds write to host process memory from malicious guest. Easy DoS, conceivably part of exploit chain. Mythos could NOT produce a functional full exploit.

name: Linux Kernel Privilege Escalation Chains
description: Multiple OOB write vulnerabilities chained (2-4 bugs per chain). Example: vuln 1 to bypass KASLR + vuln 2 to read struct + vuln 3 to write freed heap object + heap spray = root. Nearly a dozen chain examples.

name: Web Browser JIT Heap Sprays
description: Fully autonomous discovery of read/write primitives chained into JIT heap spray. One case: cross-origin bypass (attacker reads victim's bank data). Another: sandbox escape + privilege escalation = visiting a webpage gives kernel write access.

name: Cryptography Library Weaknesses
description: Vulnerabilities in the world's most popular cryptography libraries. Bug types include certificate forgery and decryption of encrypted communications. One disclosed: Botan library GHSA-v782-6fq4-q827.
